name: Pad Thai.
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the noodles for 20 to 25 minutes in enough warm water to cover. They should be soft but not easily mashed with fingers. Drain thoroughly.
2. Heat a wok, and add vegetable oil to coat the surface.
3. Add garlic and fry until golden.
4. Add shrimp (or chicken/pork) and fry until pink or until chicken/pork is no longer pink.
5. Add noodles and toss lightly to coat them with oil and distribute garlic and meat.
6. Mix fish sauce, sugar, vinegar, and tomato paste in a bowl. Stir until sugar dissolves. Set aside.
7. Slice scallions diagonally into pieces 1-1/2 inches long. Set aside.
8. Add the liquid mixture to the wok and bring to a boil rapidly, gently folding noodles without breaking them.
9. Reduce heat to medium and continue boiling, frequently folding until noodles absorb the liquid.
10. Lift noodles with a wok scoop or spatula, break one egg into oil, cover with noodles, and repeat on opposite side. Cook eggs undisturbed over moderate heat until set and nearly dry.
11. Fold eggs gently but quickly into noodles without breaking them. Toss mixture briefly to distribute evenly.
12. Add bean sprouts and scallions, tossing quickly to avoid breaking noodles. Cook for 2 minutes until beansprouts and scallions are crisp-tender.
13. Serve on a warm platter, garnishing with chilies, peanuts, and lime juice. Alternatively, serve chilies, peanuts, and lime wedges on the side for individual seasoning.
